objective: Fix engine-core startup crashes caused by zero-valued optional numeric fields being passed to vLLM as literal zeros.
scope: Apply minimal, reversible, fork-local runtime updates in src/engine_args.py and aiwf.request.json: normalize RunPod form-supplied zero values to None for optional fields where vLLM expects unset semantics.
acceptance_criteria: src/engine_args.py converts num_gpu_blocks_override=0 to None before AsyncEngineArgs creation.
src/engine_args.py converts max_parallel_loading_workers=0 to None before AsyncEngineArgs creation.
src/engine_args.py converts max_cpu_loras=0 to None before AsyncEngineArgs creation.
Normalization remains fork-local and does not change explicit non-zero values.
All edits remain fork-local, minimal, and reversible via documented rollback actions.
tests_expected: Run a fork-local RunPod smoke test with qwen/qwen3.5-27b and verify EngineCore startup no longer fails with kv_cache ZeroDivisionError
rollback_plan: Immediate rollback in this fork: revert src/engine_args.py zero-normalization lines for num_gpu_blocks_override, max_parallel_loading_workers, and max_cpu_loras; revert this two-file change set in one commit. No upstream operations required.
deploy_plan: Validate in this fork only: run py_compile + JSON checks, inspect focused diff, publish release, and perform RunPod smoke test using qwen/qwen3.5-27b with startup verification.
threat_model: Primary risk is unintentionally changing semantics for users who intentionally set 0 on optional numeric knobs. Mitigation: only normalize known optional fields where vLLM treats unset semantics as safer than literal zero in serverless startup path; keep explicit non-zero user values untouched and rollback in one commit if regressions appear.
